channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.mmorpg.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.mmorpg.com

item.title: Behaviour Interactive Lays Off Employees, Microsoft Plans Buyout Offers for 7% of Workforce
item.content:encoded:
Behaviour Interactive, the developer behind Dead By Daylight and more, has confirmed a round of layoffs. This also comes as Microsoft has a plan to offer around 7% of employees voluntary buyouts.

As for the Behaviour Interactive cuts, the company confirmed that they had laid off staff but did not mention or confirm any numbers. In a statement, a company spokesperson said, 

“A portion of Behaviour Interactive’s business has traditionally focused on external development partnerships. In recent months, demand for mobile and casual external development projects has declined. As we conclude our remaining engagements and do not foresee comparable opportunities in the near term, we have made the decision to part ways with some colleagues.”

The company says they will support affected employees in some way. Dead By Daylight continues going strong, with the company revealing a new, expanded Age of Titan collaboration.

Microsoft isn’t laying off again just yet. They’re planning to offer buyout packages to about 7% of their workforce in May, according to CNBC. These voluntary buyouts are the first time Microsoft has done this. According to the CNBC report, eligibility criteria is being at the senior director level or below, with a total combination of age and years at the company with a sum of 70 or above. May 7th looks to be the date when eligible employees will get their offers.

“Our hope is that this program gives those eligible the choice to take that next step on their own terms, with generous company support,” a memo from a Microsoft SVP says.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/behaviour-interactive-lays-off-employees-microsoft-plans-buyout-offers-for-7-of-workforce-2000137890
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Black Desert Online Arena of Solare Season Launching Next Week, NA vs. EU Showdown Regionals This Weekent
item.content:encoded:
Black Desert Online is launching the next Arena of Solare season next week, with improvements to make it more enjoyable. PvP balance changes are on the way, there’s a new hanges to quests, events, and more. Plus, we have some info on the Heidel Ball’s NA vs EU Showdown.

The new season of Arena of Solare  begins on April 30th. Pearl Abyss is planning to share more details tomorrow in the Global Lab update notice with the full list of changes, but we can expect a new ally location HUD, Some adjustments to score rules, better rewards, new battlefields, and more changes.

There will also be some class balance tweaks coming to Global Lab. The team is changing their policy of not making PVP balance changes during a regular Arena of Solare season, instead they're going to keep an eye out and make adjustments early in the season if there's some pressing changes that need to be made to relieve over or underperforming elements. 

Other changes include the new Extra AP Against Monsters (previously “against all species”)And the patch notes have more info on the  changes, but this time you'll be able to check your exact total AP against various monsters when you view total stats by using the new no species damage filter.

Some cleanup is happening for Balenos main quests after a revamp. Those with characters still on the pre-revamp questline will be able to continue on the new version.

Black Desert Online players that have logged in with Facebook won’t be able to do that anymore starting in June.

Finally, we now have info on the NA vs. EU Showdown 3v3 PvP tournament  happening at Heidel Ball, which is happening in California in honor of the 10th anniversary of Western release.  the preliminary Regional Tournament kicks off this weekend and you can watch live on stream. you can check out all of the teams participating, along with their rosters and their chosen classes here in the patch notes. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/black-desert-online-arena-of-solare-season-launching-next-week-na-vs-eu-showdown-regionals-this-weekent-2000137889
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The Quinfall is Adding a 3v3 PvP Arena and Ending PvP Zone Fees
item.content:encoded:
The Quinfall is going to add 3v3 Arena PvP and make the PvP zone free to enter in the next update. The team announced how the arena will work, some info on rewards, structure, and matchmaking, as well as tweaks to the original arena.

While there was no date for when we’ll see this update, Vawraek says that player feedback had a role in their final decision to add the new PvP option. The 3v3 arena will feature a best of three match format, with queue-based matchmaking. There will be no entry fee to participate.

Each team can have only one tank and one healer for balance reasons. Gear will be normalized to make sure it's a skills test, and there will be no consumables within the arena, including scrolls. If you win or lose, you'll still receive some kind of reward.  Vawraek is also planning to introduce a weekly league system to give highly-ranked competitors some high-value rewards. They’ll be taking action to help prevent unfair practices like win trading, and promise penalties for cheaters. 

The team is also planning on a couple of other improvements coming in the next update. The PVP Zone will be free of charge to enter, and the regular arena rewards will be increased.

Check out the rest in the 3v3 arena announcement for more details. They continue to invite feedback to help keep the experience as fair and enjoyable as possible.

The Quinfall is on 90% discount (99 cents) on Steam through May 5th.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/the-quinfall-is-adding-a-3v3-pvp-arena-and-ending-pvp-zone-fees-2000137888
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
